title: Dijamant
description:
Lovro has a table of $n$ rows and $m$ columns, where each cell is either . or #. By rotating a square by 45‚ó¶ a diamond shape is formed in the table. For a part of the table to be considered a diamond, its edge must also consist only of the character #, while its inside must be completely filled with . and it must be nonempty. Outside of a diamond any character is allowed. Diamonds come in different sizes, and the three smallest examples of a diamond are shown in the first sample.

Fabijan asked Lovro to tell him how many diamonds are there in the table, or else Lovro has to give him a cookie. Help Lovro by writing a program which counts the number of diamonds in his table.
input: The first line contains positive integers $n$ and $m$ ($1 ‚Č§ n, m ‚Č§ 2000$), the number of rows and columns. Each of the next $n$ lines contains $m$ characters . or # which describe the table.
output: In the only line print the number of diamonds in the table.
hint:
Clarification of the second example:

There is only one diamond in the table (the one with the smallest possible size). There appears to be another diamond containing it, but it is not considered a diamond because its inside is not completely filled with '.'. The shape on the right side of the table is also not a diamond because it‚Äôs missing a # character on its edge.
source: Contest, Croatian Open Competition in Informatics, COCI 2021/2022, Contest #5
